person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Wedric [Wedricus] de Condé, Seigneur de Condé, was born about 1085 CE in the Seigneurie de Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Hainaut, Empire romain germanique (Germanic Roman Empire, successor to the Empire of Charlemagne; it was declared as the "Holy Roman Empire" in the 12th Century at time of Friedrich I "Barbarossa"). He was the son of Osbert de Condé, b: 1057 and his wife (Unknown).

Unfortunately, "Medieval Lands" chose not to research the origins of the Seigneurs de Condé before Wedric's son, Roger (b: ca. 1120 CE). In fact, during the tumultuous 11th Century (1000s) when Hainaut was afflicted by several dynastic civil wars, the Seigneurie de Condé was divided and its south-eastern part near Avesnes-sur-Helpe, was ceded to a different Wedric [Wedricus ad Barbarum] called "Wedric le Sor" (Wedric the Redhead). His successors became known as the "House of Avesnes" and their part of Condé was called "Vieux Condé".

Wedric de Condé had a son: Roger de Condé , b: ca. 1120 CE in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Hainaut.

NOTE: Originally this profile had Wedric de Condé married to Mathilde (aka Mahut) de la Roche (1130 - 10 Sep 1169). Further research has shown that Mathilde de la Roche was the daughter of Henry de Namur. She married (1) Thierry de Valcourt, "célèbre & vaillant chevalier," (2) "Nicolas, Seigneur d'Avesnes, de Landrecies, de Condé et de Leuze." Consequently, I have detached her profile from .

Also, the death date of 10 Sep 1169, attributed to both Mathilde and Wedric originally was, in fact, the death date for Empress Mathilda of England, widow of King Henri I. She died 10 Sep 1169 in Rouen, Normandie, France. Thus there has been a conflation between these 2 women named "Mathilde" / "Mathilda". Wedric's death date was likely before 1170 as he would have been 85 then but the exact date and place are Unknown. Chet Snow 2 June 2019.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Wedric de Condé於1104年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾林肯時，他的父親Osbert de Cundy40歲，母親Adelaide Chesney23歲。他和Mahaut de Fontaine至少有1個兒子。他去世於1169年9月10日在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德維額 孔戴享年65歲，埋葬在法國皮卡迪艾斯孔代--布利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Condé
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Candee
meanings: Americanized form of French Condé (see Conde ) or, alternatively, Candé, a habitational name from Candé in Maine-et-Loire, or from Candé-sur-Beauvron in Loire-et-Cher, or southern French Condy .
Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. The name of the latter is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors as Jean/John Candée/Cande/Condé and in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America as John Conde. See also   Conde  .

nameMeanings.name: Wedric
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Coined by Sir Walter Scott for the character Cedric of Rotherwood in Ivanhoe ( 1819 ). It seems to be an altered form of Cerdic, the name of the traditional founder of the kingdom of Wessex. Cerdic was a Saxon (Scott's novel also has a Saxon setting), and his name is presumably of Germanic origin, but the formation is not clear. The name has acquired something of a ‘sissy’ image, partly on account of Cedric Errol Fauntleroy, the long-haired, velvet-suited boy hero of Frances Hodgson Burnett's Little Lord Fauntleroy ( 1886 ). A well-known bearer was the film actor Sir Cedric Hardwicke ( 1893–1964 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Wedric de Condé was born in 1104, in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Osbert de Cundy, was 40 and his mother, Adelaide Chesney, was 23. He had at least 1 son with Mahaut de Fontaine. He died on 10 September 1169, in Vieux Condé, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, at the age of 65, and was buried in Condé-en-Brie, Aisne, Picardie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Wedric de Condé est né en 1104, à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Osbert de Cundy, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Adelaide Chesney, avait 23 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mahaut de Fontaine. Il est décédé le 10 septembre 1169, à Vieux Condé, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Condé-en-Brie, Aisne, Picardie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Wedric de Condé 1104 in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Osbert de Cundy 40 und seine Mutter Adelaide Chesney 23. Er hatte mit Mahaut de Fontaine mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 10. September 1169 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Vieux Condé, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich und wurde in Condé-en-Brie, Aisne, Picardie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Wedric de Condé è nato il nel 1104 nel Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Osbert de Cundy, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Adelaide Chesney, aveva 23 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mahaut de Fontaine. Morì il 10 settembre 1169, nel Vieux Condé, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Condé-en-Brie, Aisne, Picardie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Wedric de Condéが1104年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアリンコルンで生まれた時，父Osbert de Cundyは40歳，母Adelaide Chesneyは23歳であった。彼はMahaut de Fontaineとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1169年9月10日にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードヴィエウックス コンドeで65歳で死去し，フランスピカーディエイスンコンドe-エン-ブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Wedric de Condé(이)가 1104년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 링코컨에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Osbert de Cundy(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelaide Chesney(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Mahaut de Fontaine(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1169년 9월 10일 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 비우스 콘드e에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 피카디 아이스니 콘드e--브리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Wedric de Condé nasceu em 1104, em Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Osbert de Cundy, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Adelaide Chesney, tinha 23 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mahaut de Fontaine. Ele faleceu em 10 de setembro de 1169, em Vieux Condé, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Condé-en-Brie, Aisne, Picardie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Wedric de Condé родился в 1104 г. в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Osbert de Cundy было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelaide Chesney, 23. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mahaut de Fontaine. Он умер 10 сентября 1169 г. в местности виеукс кондe, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности кондe-ен-бри, эисн, пикарди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Wedric de Condé nació en 1104, en Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Osbert de Cundy, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Adelaide Chesney, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mahaut de Fontaine. Murió el 10 de septiembre de 1169, en Vieux Condé, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Condé-en-Brie, Aisne, Picardie, Francia.
